perturbation.original_text: Capitalized terms not otherwise defined in this Agreement shall have the meanings ascribed to such terms in the Separation Agreement. 1.1 "Accounting Method" means U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) or, if otherwise agreed by the Parties, an alternative accounting method used in the ordinary course of business.
perturbation.changed_text: Capitalized terms not otherwise defined in this Agreement shall have the meanings ascribed to such terms in the Separation Agreement. 1.1 "Accounting Method" means an accounting method used in the ordinary course of business.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Accounting Method' specified U.S. GAAP or another method agreed upon by the parties. By removing 'U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) or, if otherwise agreed by the Parties, an alternative', the changed definition now vaguely defines accounting method, as 'an accounting method used in the ordinary course of business', which could contradict other sections that assume a more specific accounting framework. This creates uncertainty, particularly in Section 3.1(d), where the 'Standard Cost methodology' may not be consistently calculated or auditable if the baseline Accounting Method is not clearly defined or followed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) Changes to the Standard Cost Methodology. Manufacturer shall have the right to change the Standard Cost methodology once per Fiscal Year; provided that any change shall be consistent with the Accounting Method and applied across all products manufactured at the applicable Facility. If Manufacturer elects to change the Standard Cost methodology, Manufacturer shall calculate both (i) the revised Standard Cost using the methodology effective during the then-current Fiscal Year of the Term of the applicable Facility Addendum and (ii) the percentage change in Standard Cost caused by the change in methodology relative to the former methodology. If such Standard Cost methodology change results in an increase of Facility Conversion Cost for Products manufactured for Customer of more than two percent (2%), then Manufacturer shall revert to the former methodology for purposes of the calculation of Price during such Fiscal Year.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific reference to U.S. GAAP and agreement by the parties for an alternative accounting method, thus broadening the definition of 'Accounting Method' to 'an accounting method used in the ordinary course of business'. This creates a contradiction with section (d) which relies on a more specific and consistent 'Accounting Method' for calculating and auditing 'Standard Cost methodology'. The removal of GAAP or an agreed upon standard introduces ambiguity and potential for inconsistent application of the Standard Cost methodology, thus causing a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.6 Delivery; Risk of Loss. (a) Delivery. Unless otherwise set forth in the applicable Facility Addendum, Manufacturer shall deliver Product to Customer FCA (Incoterms 2010) at the applicable Facility, and all Purchase Orders will be deemed to have been completed when the quantity of Product made available to Customer at the applicable Facility is between ninety percent (90%) and one hundred and ten percent (110%) of the quantity of Product set forth in any accepted Purchase Order (each such event, a "Delivery"). Delivery shall occur by or within the delivery date(s) set forth in the applicable Purchase Order or such other date as may be agreed to in writing by the Parties from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.6 Delivery; Risk of Loss. (a) Delivery. Unless otherwise set forth in the applicable Facility Addendum, Manufacturer shall deliver Product to Customer at the applicable Facility, and all Purchase Orders will be deemed to have been completed when the quantity of Product made available to Customer at the applicable Facility is between ninety percent (90%) and one hundred and ten percent (110%) of the quantity of Product set forth in any accepted Purchase Order (each such event, a "Delivery"). Delivery shall occur by or within the delivery date(s) set forth in the applicable Purchase Order or such other date as may be agreed to in writing by the Parties from time to time.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract specified the delivery term as 'FCA (Incoterms 2010)', indicating a clear allocation of responsibility and risk during the shipping process. By removing this specific term, the modified contract now lacks a defined standard for delivery, which creates an in-text contradiction with section 2.6(c). The deletion of 'FCA (Incoterms 2010)' introduces ambiguity, and directly affects 'title to Product and risk of loss or damage', because it removes the precise term that was supposed to pass to Customer upon Delivery to Customer pursuant to Section 2.6(a)
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Title. Unless otherwise set forth in the applicable Facility Addendum, title to Product and risk of loss or damage shall pass to Customer upon Delivery to Customer pursuant to Section 2.6(a).
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'FCA (Incoterms 2010)' from Section 2.6(a) creates a contradiction with Section 2.6(c). Section 2.6(c) states that 'title to Product and risk of loss or damage shall pass to Customer upon Delivery to Customer pursuant to Section 2.6(a)'. By removing the Incoterm, the contract no longer clearly defines when and where 'Delivery' occurs for the purpose of transferring title and risk, thus creating a direct conflict and ambiguity within the document.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2 Manufacturer Covenants. Manufacturer hereby covenants to Customer that: (a) The Facility and all equipment, tooling and molds utilized in the manufacture and supply of Product hereunder by or on behalf of Manufacturer shall, during the Term of this Agreement, be maintained in good operating condition and shall be maintained and operated in accordance with all applicable Laws. The manufacturing and storage operations, procedures and processes utilized in manufacture and supply of Product hereunder (including the Facility) shall be in full compliance with all applicable Laws, including cGMP and health and safety laws.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 Manufacturer Covenants. Manufacturer hereby covenants to Customer that: (a) The Facility and all equipment, tooling and molds utilized in the manufacture and supply of Product hereunder by or on behalf of Manufacturer shall, during the Term of this Agreement, be maintained in good operating condition. The manufacturing and storage operations, procedures and processes utilized in manufacture and supply of Product hereunder shall be in full compliance with applicable Laws, including cGMP and health and safety laws.
perturbation.explanation: In the original agreement, the manufacturer had to follow applicable Laws in order to make sure all equipment, tooling and molds utilized in the manufacture and supply of Product, be maintained and operated in accordance with. By removing this section from section 5.2 (a) can create a contradiction because the manufaturer does not have a direct obligation to maintian and operate said equipment in accordance to the applicable laws. This change creates an in-text contradiction, and also weakens the assurance of compliance, which affects section 5.2 (e) as there's no assurance if Products furnished by Manufacturer to Customer under this Agreement, will be maintained and operated in accordance with the applicable Laws.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (e) Products furnished by Manufacturer to Customer under this Agreement: (i) shall be manufactured, packaged, labeled, handled, stored and Delivered in accordance with, shall be of the quality specified in, and shall conform upon Delivery to Customer (or its designee) to, the Specifications; (ii) shall be manufactured, packaged, labeled, handled, stored and Delivered in compliance with all applicable Laws including, without limitation, cGMPs, and in accordance with the Quality Agreement, this Agreement and the applicable Facility Addendum; (iii) shall not contain any Product Material that has not been used, handled or stored by or on behalf of Manufacturer in accordance with the Specifications, all applicable Laws, the Quality Agreement, this Agreement and the applicable Facility Addendum; (iv) shall not be adulterated or misbranded within the meaning of Sections 501 and 502, respectively, of the Act or any other applicable Law; and (v) shall, at the time Delivered, have at least a remaining shelf-life as specified in the applicable Facility Addendum. Notwithstanding the foregoing clauses (i) through (v) of this Section 5.2(e) or anything else contained in this Agreement or any Facility Addendum or Quality Agreement, Manufacturer shall have no liability under this Agreement (including under Section 4.11(b) or Section 10.1) or any Facility Addendum or Quality Agreement for any Non-Complying Product which is non-complying due to any Non-Complying Customer-Supplied Materials or Non-Complying Buy-Sell Materials.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the manufacturer to maintain and operate equipment in accordance with applicable laws, which provides a basis for ensuring that the products meet legal and quality standards as stated in section 5.2(e). The changed text removes the direct obligation to maintain and operate the equipment in accordance with applicable laws, weakening the assurance that products will be manufactured, packaged, labeled, handled, stored, and delivered in compliance with all applicable laws, as required in section 5.2(e). This creates a contradiction because section 5.2(e) assumes compliance with applicable laws throughout the manufacturing process, while the revised section 5.2(a) no longer explicitly mandates it for equipment maintenance and operation.
